label: Marfan Syndrome (Easy)
vignette_steps: Patient is a 16-year-old male presenting with unusually tall stature and long, slender fingers.
Ophthalmological examination reveals bilateral ectopia lentis.
Echocardiogram shows significant aortic root dilation at 4.5 cm.
Spine X-ray demonstrates mild to moderate scoliosis.
Family history: maternal uncle had aortic dissection at age 42.

label: Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy (Easy)
vignette_steps: 5-year-old boy brought in by parents concerned about difficulty climbing stairs.
Gower's sign positive. Proximal muscle weakness bilaterally.
Creatine kinase (CK) levels markedly elevated at 12,000 U/L.
Echocardiogram shows early dilated cardiomyopathy.
DMD gene deletion confirmed on MLPA.

label: Dravet Syndrome (Easy)
vignette_steps: 8-month-old female with first prolonged febrile seizure lasting 25 minutes.
Subsequent seizures occurring without fever, multiple types noted.
EEG shows generalized spike-wave discharges.
Global developmental delay apparent by 12 months.
Parents report regression in social interaction and language by 18 months.

label: Rett Syndrome (Medium)
vignette_steps: 18-month-old girl with previously normal development now showing regression in hand use.
Patient was reaching milestones appropriately until 14 months.
Stereotyped hand-wringing movements noted, loss of purposeful hand movements.
Microcephaly developing progressively.
Seizures began at 20 months. No family history of similar condition.

label: Loeys-Dietz Syndrome (Medium)
vignette_steps: 12-year-old male referred for aortic root dilation found incidentally.
Tall stature with relatively long arms and mild finger elongation.
No ectopia lentis on slit-lamp exam.
Spine MRI reveals cervical spine instability and mild scoliosis.
FBN1 negative. Family history of arterial aneurysms.

label: Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome (Medium)
vignette_steps: 19-year-old female presenting with recurrent joint dislocations since childhood.
Skin is notably soft and velvety; stretches more than 3 cm at forearm.
History of easy bruising and poor wound healing with atrophic scars.
No cardiac abnormalities on echo. Beighton score 8/9.
Mild scoliosis noted on exam. Mother has similar skin findings.

label: Achondroplasia (Medium)
vignette_steps: Newborn with short limbs noted at birth; limb shortening predominantly rhizomelic.
Head circumference at 98th percentile, relative macrocephaly.
Both parents of normal stature, de novo mutation suspected.
Trident hand configuration noted. Lumbar lordosis developing.
Skeletal survey confirms achondroplasia radiographic features.

label: Marfan with VUS Reclassification (Hard)
vignette_steps: 20-year-old female referred for palpitations. Tall with hypermobile joints.
Mild aortic root dilation (3.8 cm, z-score +2.1). No mitral valve prolapse.
FBN1 gene previously tested, reported variant of uncertain significance.
Ophthalmology now confirms subtle superotemporal lens subluxation.
VUS in FBN1 reclassified as likely pathogenic by updated ClinVar criteria.

label: Atypical Rett with Preserved Speech (Hard)
vignette_steps: 7-year-old girl with intellectual disability and autistic features, previously diagnosed with autism.
Parents note she had words at 12 months but lost most language by 24 months.
Stereotyped hand movements present but subtle.
EEG abnormal. Seizures began at age 5, responded partially to valproate.
Array-CGH and standard autism panel negative. MECP2 sequencing not yet performed.

label: Cystic Fibrosis Atypical Presentation (Hard)
vignette_steps: 3-year-old with recurrent pulmonary infections since infancy.
Failure to thrive: weight at 3rd percentile despite adequate caloric intake.
Sweat chloride test borderline at 44 mmol/L.
Standard newborn screen reported as normal.
CFTR sequencing identifies compound heterozygosity for F508del and a rare splice variant.
